nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 非法猎捕
nodes.summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 开阔山地
nodes.summary: 地形开阔且便于大翼展鸟类活动的山地区域。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: Lappet-faced vulture
nodes.summary: Lappet-faced vulture（Lappet-faced vulture，学名 Torgos tracheliotos），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，常见栖息地包括开阔山地，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、农药污染、非法猎捕。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: This vulture prefers to live in dry savannah, thornbush, arid plains, deserts with scattered trees in wadis, open mountain slopes

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The declines are almost entirely due to human activities, including disturbances from habitat destruction and cultivation, disturbances at the nesting site (to which the species is reportedly quite sensitive) and ingestion of pesticides, which are usually set out for jackals and other small mammalian carnivores

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The declines are almost entirely due to human activities, including disturbances from habitat destruction and cultivation, disturbances at the nesting site (to which the species is reportedly quite sensitive) and ingestion of pesticides, which are usually set out for jackals and other small mammalian carnivores

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: It is suspected that they died after eating the corpses of 3 elephants that were poisoned by poachers, possibly to avoid detection by the birds, which help rangers to track poaching activity by circling above where there are dead animals. On 3 April 2022 the Israel Nature and Parks Authority announced the first hatching of a lappet-faced vulture since 1994 {{Citation |title=The first lappet-faced vulture to hatch in Israel in years checks out its new home
